title: <div>Perfect. You’re asking for a rubric-based self-grading system where student
content:
Perfect. You’re asking for a rubric-based self-grading system where students:
• Evaluate their own work using letter grades (A–F)
• Justify those grades with specific examples from their draft
• Work within the same criteria structure as the formal grading rubric you provided earlier
• Reflect metacognitively on each category instead of just fixing errors

I’ll now revise the assignment to include:
1. ✅ A rubric-based self-assessment table
2. ✅ A reflection column for each row (criteria), where they defend their letter grade
3. ✅ Consistency with your original final-draft rubric layout
4. ✅ Replacement of word-count instructions with a qualitative justification model

⸻

🧠 Revised Assignment (Expanded with Self-Grading Rubric Justification)

✅ Revised Section: Rubric-Based Self-Assessment

In this section, you’ll evaluate your own draft by assigning yourself a letter grade (A, B, C, D, or F) in each of the key grading areas based on the official Compare & Contrast Essay Rubric. For each category, you must also justify your grade using examples from your current draft.
context: From document: Perfect. You’re asking for a rubric-based self-grading system where… — chunk 1 (chunk 1/6)
